verses.sanskrit: श्रीशुक उवाचस इत्थमाचरन् कामान् स्त्रैणोऽपह्नवमात्मन: ।बुद्ध्वा प्रियायै निर्विण्णो गाथामेतामगायत ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācasa ittham ācaran kāmānstraiṇo ’pahnavam ātmanaḥbuddhvā priyāyai nirviṇṇogāthām etām agāyata
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: O Mahārāja Parīkṣit, Yayāti was very much attached to woman. In due course of time, however, when disgusted with sexual enjoyment and its bad effects, he renounced this way of life and narrated the following story to his beloved wife.

verses.sanskrit: श‍ृणु भार्गव्यमूं गाथां मद्विधाचरितां भुवि ।धीरा यस्यानुशोचन्ति वने ग्रामनिवासिन: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: śṛṇu bhārgavy amūṁ gāthāṁmad-vidhācaritāṁ bhuvidhīrā yasyānuśocantivane grāma-nivāsinaḥ
verses.text: My dearly beloved wife, daughter of Śukrācārya, in this world there was someone exactly like me. Please listen as I narrate the history of his life. By hearing about the life of such a householder, those who have retired from householder life always lament.

verses.sanskrit: बस्त एको वने कश्चिद् विचिन्वन् प्रियमात्मन: ।ददर्श कूपे पतितां स्वकर्मवशगामजाम् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: basta eko vane kaścidvicinvan priyam ātmanaḥdadarśa kūpe patitāṁsva-karma-vaśagām ajām
verses.text: While wandering in the forest, eating to satisfy his senses, a he-goat by chance approached a well, in which he saw a she-goat standing helplessly, having fallen into it by the influence of the results of fruitive activities.

verses.sanskrit: तस्या उद्धरणोपायं बस्त: कामी विचिन्तयन् ।व्यधत्त तीर्थमुद्‍धृत्य विषाणाग्रेण रोधसी ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyā uddharaṇopāyaṁbastaḥ kāmī vicintayanvyadhatta tīrtham uddhṛtyaviṣāṇāgreṇa rodhasī
verses.text: After planning how to get the she-goat out of the well, the lusty he-goat dug up the earth on the well’s edge with the point of his horns in such a way that she was able to come out very easily.

verses.sanskrit: सोत्तीर्य कूपात् सुश्रोणी तमेव चकमे किल ।तया वृतं समुद्वीक्ष्य बह्व्योऽजा: कान्तकामिनी: ॥ ५ ॥पीवानं श्मश्रुलं प्रेष्ठं मीढ्‍वांसं याभकोविदम् ।स एकोऽजवृषस्तासां बह्वीनां रतिवर्धन: ।रेमे कामग्रहग्रस्त आत्मानं नावबुध्यत ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sottīrya kūpāt suśroṇītam eva cakame kilatayā vṛtaṁ samudvīkṣyabahvyo ’jāḥ kānta-kāminīḥ
verses.text: When the she-goat, who had very nice hips, got out of the well and saw the very handsome he-goat, she desired to accept him as her husband. When she did so, many other she-goats also desired him as their husband because he had a very beautiful bodily structure and a nice mustache and beard and was expert in discharging semen and in the art of sexual intercourse. Therefore, just as a person haunted by a ghost exhibits madness, the best of the he-goats, attracted by the many she-goats, engaged in erotic activities and naturally forgot his real business of self-realization.

verses.sanskrit: तमेव प्रेष्ठतमया रममाणमजान्यया ।विलोक्य कूपसंविग्ना नामृष्यद् बस्तकर्म तत् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam eva preṣṭhatamayāramamāṇam ajānyayāvilokya kūpa-saṁvignānāmṛṣyad basta-karma tat
verses.text: When the she-goat who had fallen into the well saw her beloved goat engaged in sexual affairs with another she-goat, she could not tolerate the goat’s activities.

verses.sanskrit: तं दुर्हृदं सुहृद्रूपं कामिनं क्षणसौहृदम् ।इन्द्रियाराममुत्सृज्य स्वामिनं दु:खिता ययौ ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ durhṛdaṁ suhṛd-rūpaṁkāminaṁ kṣaṇa-sauhṛdamindriyārāmam utsṛjyasvāminaṁ duḥkhitā yayau
verses.text: Aggrieved by her husband’s behavior with another, the she-goat thought that the he-goat was not actually her friend but was hardhearted and was her friend only for the time being. Therefore, because her husband was lusty, she left him and returned to her former maintainer.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽपि चानुगत: स्त्रैण: कृपणस्तां प्रसादितुम् ।कुर्वन्निडविडाकारं नाशक्नोत् पथि सन्धितुम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’pi cānugataḥ straiṇaḥkṛpaṇas tāṁ prasāditumkurvann iḍaviḍā-kāraṁnāśaknot pathi sandhitum
verses.text: Being very sorry, the he-goat, who was subservient to his wife, followed the she-goat on the road and tried his best to flatter her, but he could not pacify her.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यतत्र द्विज: कश्चिदजास्वाम्यच्छिनद् रुषा ।लम्बन्तं वृषणं भूय: सन्दधेऽर्थाय योगवित् ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya tatra dvijaḥ kaścidajā-svāmy acchinad ruṣālambantaṁ vṛṣaṇaṁ bhūyaḥsandadhe ’rthāya yogavit
verses.text: The she-goat went to the residence of a brāhmaṇa who was the maintainer of another she-goat, and that brāhmaṇa angrily cut off the he-goat’s dangling testicles. But at the he-goat’s request, the brāhmaṇa later rejoined them by the power of mystic yoga.

verses.sanskrit: सम्बद्धवृषण: सोऽपि ह्यजया कूपलब्धया ।कालं बहुतिथं भद्रे कामैर्नाद्यापि तुष्यति ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: sambaddha-vṛṣaṇaḥ so ’pihy ajayā kūpa-labdhayākālaṁ bahu-tithaṁ bhadrekāmair nādyāpi tuṣyati
verses.text: My dear wife, when the he-goat had his testicles restored, he enjoyed the she-goat he had gotten from the well, but although he continued to enjoy for many, many years, even now he has not been fully satisfied.

verses.sanskrit: तथाहं कृपण: सुभ्रु भवत्या: प्रेमयन्त्रित: ।आत्मानं नाभिजानामि मोहितस्तव मायया ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathāhaṁ kṛpaṇaḥ subhrubhavatyāḥ prema-yantritaḥātmānaṁ nābhijānāmimohitas tava māyayā
verses.text: O my dear wife with beautiful eyebrows, I am exactly like that he-goat, for I am so poor in intelligence that I am captivated by your beauty and have forgotten the real task of self-realization.

verses.sanskrit: यत् पृथिव्यां व्रीहियवं हिरण्यं पशव: स्त्रिय: ।न दुह्यन्ति मन:प्रीतिं पुंस: कामहतस्य ते ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yat pṛthivyāṁ vrīhi-yavaṁhiraṇyaṁ paśavaḥ striyaḥna duhyanti manaḥ-prītiṁpuṁsaḥ kāma-hatasya te
verses.text: A person who is lusty cannot satisfy his mind even if he has enough of everything in this world, including rice, barley and other food grains, gold, animals and women. Nothing can satisfy him.

verses.sanskrit: न जातु काम: कामानामुपभोगेन शाम्यति ।हविषा कृष्णवर्त्मेव भूय एवाभिवर्धते ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: na jātu kāmaḥ kāmānāmupabhogena śāṁyatihaviṣā kṛṣṇa-vartmevabhūya evābhivardhate
verses.text: As supplying butter to a fire does not diminish the fire but instead increases it more and more, the endeavor to stop lusty desires by continual enjoyment can never be successful. [In fact, one must voluntarily cease from material desires.]

verses.sanskrit: यदा न कुरुते भावं सर्वभूतेष्वमङ्गलम् ।समद‍ृष्टेस्तदा पुंस: सर्वा: सुखमया दिश: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yadā na kurute bhāvaṁsarva-bhūteṣv amaṅgalamsama-dṛṣṭes tadā puṁsaḥsarvāḥ sukhamayā diśaḥ
verses.text: When a man is nonenvious and does not desire ill fortune for anyone, he is equipoised. For such a person, all directions appear happy.

verses.sanskrit: या दुस्त्यजा दुर्मतिभिर्जीर्यतो या न जीर्यते ।तां तृष्णां दु:खनिवहां शर्मकामो द्रुतं त्यजेत् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yā dustyajā durmatibhirjīryato yā na jīryatetāṁ tṛṣṇāṁ duḥkha-nivahāṁśarma-kāmo drutaṁ tyajet
verses.text: For those who are too attached to material enjoyment, sense gratification is very difficult to give up. Even when one is an invalid because of old age, one cannot give up such desires for sense gratification. Therefore, one who actually desires happiness must give up such unsatisfied desires, which are the cause of all tribulations.

verses.sanskrit: मात्रा स्वस्रा दुहित्रा वा नाविविक्तासनो भवेत् ।बलवानिन्द्रियग्रामो विद्वांसमपि कर्षति ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: mātrā svasrā duhitrā vānāviviktāsano bhavetbalavān indriya-grāmovidvāṁsam api karṣati
verses.text: One should not allow oneself to sit on the same seat even with one’s own mother, sister or daughter, for the senses are so strong that even though one is very advanced in knowledge, he may be attracted by sex.

verses.sanskrit: पूर्णं वर्षसहस्रं मे विषयान् सेवतोऽसकृत् ।तथापि चानुसवनं तृष्णा तेषूपजायते ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: pūrṇaṁ varṣa-sahasraṁ meviṣayān sevato ’sakṛttathāpi cānusavanaṁtṛṣṇā teṣūpajāyate
verses.text: I have spent a full one thousand years enjoying sense gratification, yet my desire to enjoy such pleasure increases daily.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मादेतामहं त्यक्त्वा ब्रह्मण्यध्याय मानसम् ।निर्द्वन्द्वो निरहङ्कारश्चरिष्यामि मृगै: सह ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmād etām ahaṁ tyaktvābrahmaṇy adhyāya mānasamnirdvandvo nirahaṅkāraścariṣyāmi mṛgaiḥ saha
verses.text: Therefore, I shall now give up all these desires and meditate upon the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Free from the dualities of mental concoction and free from false prestige, I shall wander in the forest with the animals.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्टं श्रुतमसद्बुद्ध्वा नानुध्यायेन्न सन्दिशेत् ।संसृतिं चात्मनाशं च तत्र विद्वान् स आत्मद‍ृक् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭaṁ śrutam asad buddhvānānudhyāyen na sandiśetsaṁsṛtiṁ cātma-nāśaṁ catatra vidvān sa ātma-dṛk
verses.text: One who knows that material happiness, whether good or bad, in this life or in the next, on this planet or on the heavenly planets, is temporary and useless, and that an intelligent person should not try to enjoy or even think of such things, is the knower of the self. Such a self-realized person knows quite well that material happiness is the very cause of continued material existence and forgetfulness of one’s own constitutional position.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा नाहुषो जायां तदीयं पूरवे वय: ।दत्त्वा स्वजरसं तस्मादाददे विगतस्पृह: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktvā nāhuṣo jāyāṁtadīyaṁ pūrave vayaḥdattvā sva-jarasaṁ tasmādādade vigata-spṛhaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: After speaking in this way to his wife, Devayānī, King Yayāti, who was now free from all material desires, called his youngest son, Pūru, and returned Pūru’s youth in exchange for his own old age.

verses.sanskrit: दिशि दक्षिणपूर्वस्यां द्रुह्युं दक्षिणतो यदुम् ।प्रतीच्यां तुर्वसुं चक्र उदीच्यामनुमीश्वरम् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: diśi dakṣiṇa-pūrvasyāṁdruhyuṁ dakṣiṇato yadumpratīcyāṁ turvasuṁ cakraudīcyām anum īśvaram
verses.text: King Yayāti gave the southeast to his son Druhyu, the south to his son Yadu, the west to his son Turvasu, and the north to his son Anu. In this way he divided the kingdom.

verses.sanskrit: भूमण्डलस्य सर्वस्य पूरुमर्हत्तमं विशाम् ।अभिषिच्याग्रजांस्तस्य वशे स्थाप्य वनं ययौ ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhū-maṇḍalasya sarvasyapūrum arhattamaṁ viśāmabhiṣicyāgrajāṁs tasyavaśe sthāpya vanaṁ yayau
verses.text: Yayāti enthroned his youngest son, Pūru, as the emperor of the entire world and the proprietor of all its riches, and he placed all the other sons, who were older than Pūru, under Pūru’s control.

verses.sanskrit: आसेवितं वर्षपूगान् षड्‍वर्गं विषयेषु स: ।क्षणेन मुमुचे नीडं जातपक्ष इव द्विज: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: āsevitaṁ varṣa-pūgānṣaḍ-vargaṁ viṣayeṣu saḥkṣaṇena mumuce nīḍaṁjāta-pakṣa iva dvijaḥ
verses.text: Having enjoyed sense gratification for many, many years, O King Parīkṣit, Yayāti was accustomed to it, but he gave it up entirely in a moment, just as a bird flies away from the nest as soon as its wings have grown.

verses.sanskrit: स तत्र निर्मुक्तसमस्तसङ्गआत्मानुभूत्या विधुतत्रिलिङ्ग: ।परेऽमले ब्रह्मणि वासुदेवेलेभे गतिं भागवतीं प्रतीत: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa tatra nirmukta-samasta-saṅgaātmānubhūtyā vidhuta-triliṅgaḥpare ’male brahmaṇi vāsudevelebhe gatiṁ bhāgavatīṁ pratītaḥ
verses.text: Because King Yayāti completely surrendered unto the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Vāsudeva, he was freed from all contamination of the material modes of nature. Because of his self-realization, he was able to fix his mind upon the Transcendence [Parabrahman, Vāsudeva], and thus he ultimately achieved the position of an associate of the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा गाथां देवयानी मेने प्रस्तोभमात्मन: ।स्त्रीपुंसो: स्‍नेहवैक्लव्यात् परिहासमिवेरितम् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutvā gāthāṁ devayānīmene prastobham ātmanaḥstrī-puṁsoḥ sneha-vaiklavyātparihāsam iveritam
verses.text: When Devayānī heard Mahārāja Yayāti’s story of the he-goat and she-goat, she understood that this story, which was presented as if a funny joke for entertainment between husband and wife, was intended to awaken her to her constitutional position.

verses.sanskrit: सा सन्निवासं सुहृदां प्रपायामिव गच्छताम् ।विज्ञायेश्वरतन्त्राणां मायाविरचितं प्रभो: ॥ २७ ॥सर्वत्र सङ्गमुत्सृज्य स्वप्नौपम्येन भार्गवी ।कृष्णे मन: समावेश्य व्यधुनोल्लिङ्गमात्मन: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā sannivāsaṁ suhṛdāṁprapāyām iva gacchatāmvijñāyeśvara-tantrāṇāṁmāyā-viracitaṁ prabhoḥ
verses.text: Thereafter, Devayānī, the daughter of Śukrācārya, understood that the materialistic association of husband, friends and relatives is like the association in a hotel full of tourists. The relationships of society, friendship and love are created by the māyā of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, exactly as in a dream. By the grace of Kṛṣṇa, Devayānī gave up her imaginary position in the material world. Completely fixing her mind upon Kṛṣṇa, she achieved liberation from the gross and subtle bodies.

verses.sanskrit: नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते वासुदेवाय वेधसे ।सर्वभूताधिवासाय शान्ताय बृहते नम: ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: namas tubhyaṁ bhagavatevāsudevāya vedhasesarva-bhūtādhivāsāyaśāntāya bṛhate namaḥ
verses.text: O Lord Vāsudeva, O Supreme Personality of Godhead, You are the creator of the entire cosmic manifestation. You live as the Supersoul in everyone’s heart and are smaller than the smallest, yet You are greater than the greatest and are all-pervading. You appear completely silent, having nothing to do, but this is due to Your all-pervading nature and Your fullness in all opulences. I therefore offer my respectful obeisances unto You.
